Title: Identification of an algal xylan synthase indicates that there is functional orthology between algal and plant cell wall biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; the only protein group enzymatically implicated in 1,4-β-xylan backbone synthesis in plants according to the paper
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylosyl residues from UDP-xylose to xylooligosaccharide acceptors to elongate a β-1,4-xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major structural cell wall polysaccharide; implicated in plant cell wall architecture and terrestrialization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell wall, especially luminal side/newly synthesized wall region in K. flaccidum cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes synthesis of substituted 1,4-β-xylan in K. flaccidum cell walls; enzyme displays exclusive β-1,4-xylan synthase activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic analysis; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and HEK293 cells; affinity purification; xylosyltransferase enzyme assays with UDP-Xyl and xylooligosaccharide acceptors; HPLC; MALDI-TOF-MS; 1D 1H NMR; xylanase digestion specificity; cell wall monosaccharide composition; PACE; CoMPP; immunolabeling
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi membrane protein predicted by N-terminal transmembrane domain; recombinant soluble/truncated protein secreted in HEK293 assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1/GAL10 galactose-inducible promoter in pESC-URA for yeast expression; mammalian transient expression vector pGEn2 in HEK293 cells
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous expression systems and in vitro enzyme assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome mining using Klebsormidium flaccidum genome and 1KP transcriptome/proteome resources
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for engineering xylan backbone biosynthesis and modifying cell wall polysaccharide composition in plants/algae; provides an evolutionarily conserved xylan synthase for synthetic biology
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named Klebsormidium flaccidum XYLAN SYNTHASE-1. Shares 75% amino acid identity across 376 residues with AtXYS1. No activity detected with UDP-glucose, UDP-Arap, or UDP-Araf. NMR excluded 1,3-β-xylan formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KfXYS1 is an algal ortholog of plant IRX10/XYS1 identified in charophyte algae. 2. Purified KfXYS1 catalyzes successive transfer of Xyl residues from UDP-Xyl to generate β-1,4-linked xylan oligosaccharides in two heterologous systems. 3. Product structural analyses by xylanase digestion, MALDI-TOF-MS, and NMR demonstrated exclusive 1,4-β-xylan synthase activity. 4. K. flaccidum cell walls contain substituted 1,4-β-xylan, supporting physiological relevance and functional orthology with land-plant xylan synthases.
